excerpt: All disputes concerning the interpretation and/or application of the terms of this Agreement shall be submitted, if at all, to the grievance/arbitration procedure as called for herein. Failure to initially pursue grievance/arbitration in these instances shall be the basis for a plea in abatement in response to any suit or claim filed with a court of law and/or administrative agency.
notes: Grievance/contract-interpretation arbitration under Chapter 174; not a Sec.174.1535 compulsory interest-arbitration clause. Already captured verbatim in contracts.csv arbitration_clause_text this session -- reproduced here, not re-extracted.

excerpt: The City hereby recognizes the Association as the sole and exclusive collective bargaining agent for the unit consisting of all sworn Police Officers, except the Chief.
notes: Recovered via bounded OCR 2026-07-10 (previously unreadable, text_quality=partial); source now text_quality=ocr_messy.

excerpt: The Association shall not cause, counsel, or permit its members to strike, slow down, disrupt, impede or otherwise impair the normal functions of the Department; nor refuse to cross any picket line by whoever established, where such refusal would interfere with or impede the performance of the Officer's duties as an employee of the City. The City shall not lock out any Officer.

Subject to the terms of this Agreement, the Association recognizes the management of the City of San Antonio and the direction of the Police Department are vested exclusively in the City, and nothing in this Agreement is intended to circumscribe or modify the existing right of the City to operate and manage its affairs in all respects.
notes: Recovered via bounded OCR 2026-07-10. Whitespace normalized from OCR line breaks; wording otherwise verbatim.

excerpt: The arbitrator shall not have the power to add to, amend, modify, or subtract from the provisions of this Agreement in arriving at his/her decision on the issue or issues presented and shall confine his/her decision to the interpretation of this Agreement.
notes: Recovered via bounded OCR 2026-07-10. Grievance/contract-interpretation arbitration (AAA rules, final and binding) -- distinct from the separate Impasse Procedure mechanism below.

excerpt: In the event the City and the Association reach an impasse in collective bargaining negotiations, as such impasse is defined in Chapter 174 of the Local Government Code, the parties shall abide by the impasse procedure set forth in City Ordinance No. 51838, which ordinance is set forth in Attachment 3 hereto and incorporated herein by reference.
notes: Recovered via bounded OCR 2026-07-10. Chapter 174 impasse-to-ordinance-procedure, distinct from Sec.174.1535 compulsory interest arbitration (San Antonio is below that threshold's population trigger).

excerpt: A comparison of wages, hours and conditions of employment of San Antonio Police Officers and Firefighters with the wages, hours, and conditions of employment of other public and private employees in the local labor market area performing similar services and with other employees generally in public and private employ- ment in comparable cities in the State of Texas and the City of San Antonio
notes: Recovered via bounded OCR 2026-07-10. Genuine peer/comparator wage-comparability criterion embedded in the ordinance-defined factfinding process invoked by the Impasse Procedure article -- the first confirmed comparator-wage language found for San Antonio in this project's corpus.

excerpt: Disciplinary matters which are subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission pursuant to Chapter 143 Local Government Code are not subject to this procedure but are covered in Article 28 of this Agreement.
